query: FAA proposed rule supersonic overland flight July 2026 duty rest requirements transport category airplane certification streamlining emergency airworthiness directive MD-10 DC-10 2026
a_issues: Content A misses key primary-source results for several parts of the query. It has no direct result for the July 2026 FAA duty/rest NPRM and no direct result for the transport-category airplane certification modernization NPRM. Its supersonic coverage is mostly secondary sources, plus one low-information Facebook hit, which is less reliable than Federal Register or DOT sources. The emergency AD result is present but not clearly tied in the snippet to MD-10/DC-10 details beyond a partial list.
b_issues: Content B still lacks the transport-category airplane certification modernization NPRM result from June 26, 2026, so it does not fully cover every component of the query. Some results are secondary sources, and the Crowell roundup compresses multiple actions into one summary rather than giving the primary document for the duty/rest NPRM. The emergency AD result does not surface the full directive details in the snippet, but it correctly identifies MD-10/DC-10 among affected models.
reasoning: B better enables a correct answer because it contains reliable, directly relevant sources for more of the query’s components. For supersonic overland flight, B includes the Federal Register primary source dated July 2, 2026, matching the anchor and giving the key fact that FAA proposed replacing the overland ban with a noise/performance-based framework. For the July 2026 duty/rest item, B at least includes a result explicitly stating FAA proposes to clarify that federal duty/rest rules preempt state and local meal/rest break requirements, which aligns with the anchor. For the MD-10/DC-10 emergency airworthiness directive, B includes a DOT result explicitly listing DC-10 and MD-10 models. By contrast, A lacks the duty/rest NPRM entirely, lacks the certification-streamlining NPRM entirely, and relies heavily on secondary or weak sources for the supersonic rule. Neither set fully covers the transport-category certification modernization item, but B covers more of the asked facts with better primary-source support.
